LAW AND MOTION 302, CLAIMANT ADIL HIRAMANEK'S LEAVE / MOTION TO TAKE NOTICE OF, AND ENTER NOTICE OF LIEN ON JUDGMENT - PURPORTED LIEN CLAIMANT ADIL HIRAMANEK'S REQUEST FOR A COURT ORDER FOR A "RIGHT TO ATTACH" LIEN TO THE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE SOLE AUTHORITY CITED FOR MR. HIRAMENEK'S REQUEST IS CCP 491.410, BUT THAT CODE SECTION ONLY APPLIES IF THE PURPORTED LIEN CLAIMANT (HERE MR. HIRAMENEK) HAS ALREADY OBTAINED AN ATTACHMENT, WHICH MR. HIRAMENEK HAS NOT DONE. TO THE EXTENT THAT HIRAMENEK IS SEEKING AN ATTACHMENT, HE HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THE PROBABLE VALIDITY OF HIS CLAIM THAT PLAINTIFF EDDIE SUMMERFIELD OWES HIM 50% OF THE JUDGMENT ENTERED IN THIS CASE. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. Appearances:
Thomas Bloom, Esq., Law Office of Thomas M. Bloom, 831.227.9142, appearing for and with Plaintiff Eddie Summerfield;
Adil Hiramanek, Claimant/Creditor in pro per, 408.673.7560, appears by CourtCall.

CLAIMANT ADIL HIRAMANEK'S LEAVE / MOTION TO TAKE NOTICE OF, AND ENTER NOTICE OF LIEN ON JUDGMENT.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted: Purported lien claimant Adil Hiramanek's request for a court order for a "right to attach" lien to the judgment is denied. The sole authority cited for Mr. Hiramenek's request is CCP 491.410, but that code section only applies if the purported lien claimant (here Mr. Hiramenek) has already obtained an attachment, which Mr. Hiramenek has not done. To the extent that Hiramenek is seeking an attachment, he has failed to provide admissible evidence establishing the probable validity of his claim that plaintiff Eddie Summerfield owes him 50% of the judgment entered in this case. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants David Graff And Guizhen Luo To Set Aside And To Vacate Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ISMAEL D. PEREZ, TEL. NO. 1-408-293-7100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. KATHARINE CHAO OF CHAO LEGAL, TEL. NO. 1-415-738-5352 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DAVID GRAFF and GUIZHEN LUO'S Motion To Set Aside And To Vacate Entry Of Default is GRANTED. Declarations establish Defendants were more likely than not unaware of the lawsuit against them until May 4, 2015, and were never served, personally or otherwise. Vacating the judgment is therefore appropriate under Code of Civil Procedure 473(d) and/or 473.5. The Court rejects Defendant's argument that there are insufficient contacts between Defendants and California to support the constitutional exercise of jurisdiction. Defendants admit that Defendant Graff visited California in March 2008 to speak with Plaintiff about the fiance visa process; Plaintiff's Declaration alleges that he paid Graff $10,000 at this meeting and was the victim of fraud as a result, and that Defendant Luo was a participant in the fraud as well. A state has a special interest in exercising judicial jurisdiction over those who commit torts within its territory. (Keeton v. Hustler Magazine, Inc. (1984) 465 U.S. 770, quoting Leeper v. Leeper (1974) 114 N.H. 294, 298.) Defendants shall file a responsive pleading to the Complaint within 15 days. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
